game_name: Spider-Man and the X-Men in Arcade's Revenge

danger_signals.visual_form: Simulated deadly game arenas with hazardous obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat zone; player character is in immediate danger from Arcade's traps

danger_signals.visual_form: Villain character model and dialogue boxes announcing game events
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; communicates the orchestrated nature of danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Cyclops, Storm, Wolverine, and Gambit absent from overworld; their disappearance announced through narrative text
danger_signals.meaning: World state of crisis; heroes are in distress and require rescue, establishing high-stakes scenario

danger_signals.visual_form: Guardians/obstacles that must be deactivated in correct sequence (indicated by Spider-Sense)
danger_signals.meaning: Puzzle-based danger; failure to sequence correctly extends threat exposure

safety_signals.visual_form: Mechanic that indicates correct order for Security Eyes deactivation
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and guidance; suggests Spider-Man has protective awareness

safety_signals.visual_form: Progression through character event stages (two events per hero)
safety_signals.meaning: Achievement of safety milestones; advancing toward final confrontation and escape

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple playable X-Men characters available for selection
safety_signals.meaning: Redundancy and support; allies provide tactical options for survival

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban city setting (swinging through the city); abandoned building as entry point to Murderworld
Placement within Murderworld arenas; each hero assigned two themed 'events' (level pairs)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Direct opposition to heroes; sole captor/orchestrator
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Abandoned building headquarters; Murderworld simulator; control of garbage truck (tool of abduction)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spider-Man notices absences of Cyclops, Storm, Wolverine; witnesses Gambit being sucked into garbage truck
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spider-Man initiates pursuit; shifts from observer to active responder

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to abandoned building; interior shifts to simulated deadly game arenas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to complete hero-specific 'events' in sequence; all heroes share single life pool, linking fates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each character's two-stage event arc; Security Eyes puzzles requiring Spider-Sense guidance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must select and complete each hero's first event before unlocking second; branching but mandatory path structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spider-Man becomes playable for final battle with Arcade
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shift from multi-character cooperative puzzle-solving to single-hero combat; escape is conditional on Arcade defeat

environmental_storytelling.example: Opening scenes show X-Men disappearances and Gambit's abduction via garbage truck—establishes world threat without cutscene, using gameplay event
Abandoned building as Arcade's headquarters communicates villain preference for isolated, controlled environments; Murderworld interior signals artificial/simulated space
Spider-Sense guidance system communicates Spider-Man's unique sensory advantage; positions him as leader/detective in rescue mission
All heroes sharing single life pool visually communicates their interdependence and unified peril under Arcade's control

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Swings through city, notices disappearances, pursues captor; demonstrates agency and protective motivation
Trapped within Murderworld events; must complete assigned challenges; absence from overworld signals captivity
Designs and executes death traps; communicates through dialogue; orchestrates sequential challenges; presents as untouchable until final stage
Must be deactivated in Spider-Sense-indicated order; failure locks progression; suggests Arcade's systems are puzzle-based rather than raw force

notes: This 1992 platformer communicates world-state primarily through narrative sequence and level progression structure rather than dense visual design. The core tension is established through absence (missing X-Men) and containment (Murderworld prison). Character factions are differentiated by role (captor vs. captives vs. rescuer) rather than visual iconography. The shared life pool is a critical manifestation mechanic—it transforms the game from individual hero challenges into a unified survival narrative. Spider-Sense as a guidance system is thematically coherent with Spider-Man's canonical abilities, reinforcing hero identity through mechanic. The game's communication paradigm relies on early 1990s platformer conventions: level progression as narrative beat, character availability as state indicator, arena design as environmental storytelling.
